RIDGEVIEW HEALTHCARE & REHAB CENTER

Overview

200 PENNSYLVANIA AVENUE, SHENANDOAH, PA 17976

Setting
Rural
—
Rating not available

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
Footnote 18.
— Not Available
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
Footnote 18.
— Not Available
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Footnote 18.
— Not Available
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
Footnote 18.
— Not Available

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.39 3.45 3.80 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.66 0.60 0.74 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.72 0.76 0.81 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.01 2.08 2.25 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.38 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.02 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.93
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.30
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.29
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
72.4%
annual
RN turnover
52.9%
annual
Administrators departed
2
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.22
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.89
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-12-03 15 8 7 68 1 68
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2025-05-23 54 4 50 424 1 424
Total weighted health score
157.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (73)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

2 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
68 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
3 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2025-12-03 2025-12-04
0575 Post a list of names, addresses, and telephone numbers of all pertinent State agencies and advocacy groups and a statement that the resident may file a complaint with the State Survey Agency. C 2025-12-03 2025-12-04
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-12-03 2025-12-04
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. D 2025-12-03 2025-12-04
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-12-03 2025-12-04
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-12-03 2025-12-04
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-12-03 2025-12-04
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-12-03 2025-12-04
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-05-23 2025-07-08
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-05-23 2025-07-08
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-05-23 2025-07-08
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. E 2025-05-23 2025-07-08
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2025-03-13 2025-04-29
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2025-03-13 2025-04-29
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-03-13 2025-04-29
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-03-13 2025-04-29
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. D 2025-03-13 2025-04-29
0836 Ensure the facility is licensed under applicable State and local law and operates and provides services in compliance with all applicable Federal, State, and local laws, regulations, and codes, and with accepted professional standards. E 2025-03-13 2025-04-29
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-03-13 2025-04-29
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. G 2025-01-22 2025-03-13
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-12-20 2025-03-13
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-12-20 2025-03-13
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. F 2024-12-20 2025-03-13
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-12-20 2025-04-29
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. E 2024-12-20 2025-03-13
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-12-20 2025-04-29
0836 Ensure the facility is licensed under applicable State and local law and operates and provides services in compliance with all applicable Federal, State, and local laws, regulations, and codes, and with accepted professional standards. E 2024-12-20 2025-04-29
0882 Designate a qualified infection preventionist to be responsible for the infection prevent and control program in the nursing home. F 2024-09-10 2024-10-15
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2024-07-26 2024-10-15
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-07-26 2024-10-15
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. G 2024-07-26 2024-10-15
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-07-26 2024-10-15
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2024-07-26 2024-10-15
0809 Ensure meals and snacks are served at times in accordance with resident’s needs, preferences, and requests. Suitable and nourishing alternative meals and snacks must be provided for residents who want to eat at non-traditional times or outside of scheduled meal times. E 2024-07-26 2024-10-15
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2024-07-26 2024-10-15
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-06-26 2024-07-16
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. E 2024-06-26 2024-07-16
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-06-26 2024-07-16
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-04-24 2024-06-18
0559 Honor the resident's right to share a room with spouse or roommate of choice and receive written notice before a change is made. E 2024-04-24 2024-06-18
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2024-04-24 2024-06-18
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-04-24 2024-06-18
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-04-24 2024-07-16
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2024-04-24 2024-07-16
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2024-04-24 2024-06-18
0809 Ensure meals and snacks are served at times in accordance with resident’s needs, preferences, and requests. Suitable and nourishing alternative meals and snacks must be provided for residents who want to eat at non-traditional times or outside of scheduled meal times. E 2024-04-24 2024-06-18

Deficiencies 0809 — description Ensure meals and snacks are served at times in accordance with resident’s needs, preferences, and requests. Deficiencies 0809 — description Ensure meals and snacks are served at times in accordance with resident’s needs, preferences, and requests.
